guidance: Use this exact name for the public GitHub repository unless changed before first release.

statement: Public Yahoo Finance API reference and change-tracking project.
guidance: The repository documents observed behavior, fields, schemas, endpoint outputs, market states, and changes over time.

statement: Provide supporting tools, templates, and validation scripts for repeatable public observation.
guidance: Scripts support documentation and evidence gathering; they are not the product itself.

statement: Not primarily an app-development or app-debugging project.
guidance: Third-party apps may be tested as black boxes, but the project is broader than any one app.

statement: Public users, data collectors, researchers, spreadsheet users, analysts, and developers who need to understand Yahoo Finance output behavior.
guidance: Write documentation for non-developers first, with technical detail available in tables and appendices.

statement: All claims should be tied to observed captures, timestamps, symbols, endpoints, and result classifications.
guidance: Prefer raw capture preservation plus summarized interpretation.

statement: Use evidence statuses such as observed, confirmed, needs_retest, deprecated, and rejected.
guidance: Avoid treating one-off anomalies as permanent API facts until retested.

statement: This is not an official Yahoo or Yahoo Finance project.
guidance: Do not imply endorsement, API guarantees, or official documentation status.

statement: App developers may later participate as contributors or users of the public reference.
guidance: Their participation should strengthen the public reference without changing the project into an app-support repository.

statement: Small, evidence-labeled releases are preferred over large undocumented changes.
guidance: Each release should explain what changed, what was observed, and what still needs retesting.
